input.command:
coop-send agent1 --type "DEFER" --files "src/jinja2/ext.py" --spec "$(cat <<'EOF'
Feature: optional trans-tag metadata (note/created/modified) stored on the Output node as trans_metadata, for translation-tooling consumption. No effect on rendering or babel_extract.

1) In InternationalizationExtension.parse(), inside the loop that parses trans-tag parameters (the loop that currently builds `variables` dict from NAME tokens, handling `NAME` and `NAME=expr` forms, e.g. around where it does `parser.stream.expect(...)`, reads a Name via `parser.parse_assign_target(name_only=True)` or similar, then checks for `=` to parse an expr):
   - Add `metadata: t.Dict[str, str] = {}` initialized alongside `variables = {}` before the loop.
   - For each parsed parameter name, check `if name in ("note", "created", "modified")`:
     - If the next token is `=` (assignment present): parse the value expression as before (via `parser.parse_expression()`), then require it be a string literal: `isinstance(value, nodes.Const) and isinstance(value.value, str)`. If not, raise:
       `parser.fail(f"metadata parameter {name!r} must be a string literal.")`
       (use `self.fail` or `parser.fail` — match whatever the existing code uses for other errors in this method; it should surface as TemplateAssertionError like other parse() failures in this method do)
       On success: `metadata[name] = value.value` and do NOT add it to `variables`.
     - If there is no `=` (bare name): raise
       `parser.fail(f"metadata parameter {name!r} requires a value assignment.")`
   - Any other NAME (not note/created/modified) keeps existing behavior — treated as a regular variable, assignable or bare, exactly as today (including your `domain` handling and plain variables like `user_count`).
   - Metadata parameters may appear anywhere in the comma-separated parameter list, interleaved with variables, `trimmed`/`notrimmed`, and (per your feature) `domain=...` — order must not matter.

2) In `_make_node()`:
   - Add a new parameter `metadata: t.Dict[str, str]` to the signature (I don't know your exact final signature after adding `domain`; just add `metadata` as one more parameter, e.g. keyword with no default, and thread it through from the two call sites in parse() — the singular and pluralized call sites — passing the `metadata` dict built in step 1).
   - Near the end of `_make_node()`, right before it returns the `Output` node (or wraps it), after the output_node is constructed:
     ```
     if metadata:
         output_node.trans_metadata = metadata
     ```
   - Do NOT set the attribute at all when `metadata` is empty (i.e., no `output_node.trans_metadata = {}` — the attribute must be entirely absent, so `hasattr(node, "trans_metadata")` is False for metadata-less trans tags).
   - This must not alter what gets rendered — `trans_metadata` is purely an extra attribute on the node object, not part of the rendered nodes/output list.

3) Backward compatibility / integration:
   - Existing trans tag syntax (variables, trimmed/notrimmed, pluralize) must work unchanged when no metadata params are given.
   - babel_extract must continue to work unchanged (it doesn't need to know about trans_metadata).
   - Empty string is a valid metadata value (`note=""`) — must not be rejected.
   - Unicode / long strings in metadata values must work fine (no extra validation needed beyond "is a string Const").

Please echo back confirmation once integrated so I can verify with tests on my end.
EOF
)"

coop-send agent1 --type "DEFER" --files "src/jinja2/ext.py" --spec "Already sent the full trans_metadata SPEC in my previous message (timestamp ~1783509... right after your first CLAIM) — please check coop-recv history for the DEFER with the detailed spec (parse() loop changes for note/created/modified, string-literal validation, exact parser.fail messages, metadata dict threading into _make_node, and the `if metadata: output_node.trans_metadata = metadata` step). Confirming here: agent1 owns src/jinja2/ext.py, I will not edit it. Let me know once integrated." --note "confirming ownership + pointing to prior spec message"
